title: Structural Engineer

rationales.taskAutomation: AI can run preliminary load-path analysis and draft standard structural calc packages, but stamped design responsibility and seismic/wind risk judgment stay with the licensed engineer.
rationales.toolMaturity: Structural-analysis software with AI-assisted optimization plug-ins is credible but not yet a mature, universal standard.
rationales.adoption: Larger structural firms use AI-assisted analysis tools on complex projects; most routine building work still runs traditional calc-and-review cycles.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can run structural-analysis batches and flag code-check failures, but stamped drawing sign-off and forensic failure investigation need a licensed engineer.

tasksGrowing: Stamped drawing sign-off and licensed design responsibility
Seismic and wind-risk judgment for novel structures
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 17-2051, p25-p75 rounded
insight: Load-path analysis and calc-package drafting are increasingly AI-assisted, but the stamp on a structural drawing, and the legal liability it carries, still belongs to a licensed engineer.
